notes: Solflare is listed among major wallets that support Token Extensions, which includes fee-on-transfer tokens. No Solflare-specific doc confirms the exact send-flow fee display.
Solflare detects and warns when a transaction changes token account authority/delegation. This is the SPL approval flow rather than a documented permanentDelegate flag, but it is the closest analog.
Solflare supports Token Extensions generally; transfer-hook sends build extra accounts but are typically surfaced with a warning (mechanical inference, no per-extension doc).
Display-only extension; Solflare renders Token-2022 mints (not separately documented per extension).
Display-only UI scaling; expected to render (low confidence, not separately documented).
Solflare renders Token-2022 token metadata for display.
Solflare displays name/symbol/image from on-chain metadata (implied by general Token Extensions support).
Account-level extension; expected to be honored as part of general Token Extensions support (low confidence).
Token may render but confidential balance display is unverified; encrypted balances do not render as plaintext.
If accounts default to frozen, sends fail until thawed (mechanical inference).
Sends fail while the mint is paused (mechanical inference).
Non-transferable is a protocol-level restriction: the Token-2022 program rejects the Transfer instruction, so a send from Solflare fails regardless of wallet.
Solflare renders Token-2022 mints; an unrecognized extension still displays as a balance (no documented block).

Solflare publicly supports Token-2022 / Token Extensions and is consistently grouped with Phantom and Backpack as a supporting wallet, but it does NOT publish a per-extension behavior document the way Phantom does.
As a result almost all Solflare verdicts are low confidence — based on general support claims plus protocol-level reasoning. Its clearest documented risk flag is the delegation/approval warning.
